results.title: The Gemini app becomes more agentic, delivering proactive, 24/7 ...
results.content:
It goes far beyond a simple summary. Daily Brief actively organizes and prioritizes based on your specific goals, even suggesting immediate next steps. You can easily steer it by giving responses a quick thumbs up or down over time.

Daily Brief begins rolling out today to Google AI Plus, Pro and Ultra subscribers, starting in the U.S.

 

## Gemini Spark: From information to action

We’re also introducing Gemini Spark, a 24/7 personal AI agent that helps you navigate your digital life. Spark represents a big shift for Gemini, transforming it from an assistant that can answer your questions into an active partner that does real work on your behalf and under your direction. [...] Gemini 3.5 Flash: The first in our next generation of models that combines frontier intelligence with lightning-fast action.
 Neural Expressive: A vibrant, dynamic and completely reimagined design language for Gemini.
 Gemini Omni: Our new model that can seamlessly transform text, images and video prompts into cinematic, high-quality video outputs.
 Daily Brief: A new agent that gives you a personalized morning brief and organizes exactly what you need to know to start your day.
 Gemini Spark: A 24/7 personal AI agent designed to proactively manage tasks and help you navigate your digital life, all under your direction.
 MacOS app: Our desktop app will be integrating Gemini Spark so it can operate on your local machine, and it will also add powerful new voice features.

results.title: What is Google Gemini Spark?Your 24/7 personal AI agent. - Medium
results.content:
The learning part sounds fancy but it’s straightforward.

Spark does not retrain its underlying AI on your data. That would be expensive and slow. Instead it builds a separate layer of context about you. This layer sits on top of the base model and tells it how you specifically do things.

What this means in practice:

   The base model knows how to draft an email
   Your personal layer knows you start emails with “Hi” not “Hello”
   Your personal layer knows you never use exclamation points
   Your personal layer knows you copy your manager on anything about budget

Over time this personal layer gets more accurate. Spark’s actions feel less like a robot and more like you.

### Privacy Questions

A thing that watches everything you do will make you nervous. It should. [...] Gemini Spark is proactive. It runs all the time. It connects to your calendar, your email, your documents. It watches what you do. Then it starts doing some of those things for you.

Think of it less like a chatbot and more like a background process that can think.

Examples of what Spark can do:

   Move receipts into an “Expenses” folder after noticing you always do this
   Pre-load a dashboard summary every Monday because you review it then
   Draft responses to emails based on how you’ve replied to similar messages in the past

This is not science fiction. This is shipping now.

### How It Learns

The learning part sounds fancy but it’s straightforward. [...] Gmail (drafting, filtering, summarizing)
   Google Calendar (scheduling, reminders, conflict detection)
   Google Drive and Docs (organization, content generation)
   Google Meet (transcription, summaries, follow-up tasks)
   Google Tasks and Keep (contextual to-dos)

If you live in Google Workspace, Spark lives there too.

### Third Party Connections

External tools come next. Slack, Salesforce, Notion.

The early integrations can read data from these tools. Writing to them or triggering actions inside them is harder. That will come later.

The real test is whether Spark can truly act across the tools you actually use, not just the ones Google owns.

### The Bigger Picture

Spark is not Google’s only agent. They have several.

## Get Tahir’s stories in your inbox

results.title: Gemini Spark – Your 24/7 personal AI agent for productivity
results.content:
Try Gemini

# Gemini Spark Your 24/7 personal AI agent.

Gemini Spark helps you navigate your digital life. Give it a task and it works in the background 24/7, even if your phone and laptop are turned off. It operates autonomously, but always under your direction. You choose to turn it on and it's designed to check with you before taking major actions.

Help me find and track interior design internships in New Orleans for this summer.

Help me find and track interior design internships in New Orleans for this summer.

 

Every Monday at 9:00 AM, scan my inbox and review my emails from the past week. Give me a quick recap of the most important updates and provide a suggested, prioritized to-do list for this week. Also schedule some calendar blocks for deep work. [...] While standard chatbots wait for a prompt, Gemini Spark can take proactive action on your behalf, under your direction. It doesn't just answer questions, it completes multi-step tasks across your applications making it a true AI agent rather than just a conversational tool.

Absolutely. It is a highly capable AI agent for email management. You can instruct it to draft replies, sort incoming messages based on priority, or extract action items from long threads, making it the perfect AI agent for personal use when you're overwhelmed by unread messages. [...] Not at all. We designed Gemini Spark to be an accessible AI agent for everyone. You interact with it using regular conversation. Whether you're setting up complex, multi-app schedules or just using it as a personal AI agent for your daily tasks, no technical expertise is required.

Check responses. Supervise closely, interrupt when needed. Subscription required. Compatibility and availability vary. 18+.

results.title: I put Google's 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and ...
results.content:
## Room to improve

While Spark already performed fairly well on my tasks with only small quibbles, the biggest criticism I had was that there’s no need for this to be a stand-alone product with a different branding. I think that adds to consumer confusion in this day and age, where there are so many things happening in the AI space, and where every new model has its own name and number, and some of these are quite wild. (Nano Banana, anyone?)

Image 12

Image Credits:Gemini screenshot by TechCrunch [...] I also think the lack of Keep integration is a major miss in terms of being helpful with your personal productivity. Google Docs is overkill for a packing list. And, unfortunately for iPhone users, tapping into Gemini Spark directly from your device through a push of a hardware button or gesture won’t be possible — unless Apple announces this in June at WWDC? Instead, you’ll need to launch the Gemini app and use it from there. (Another issue with having Spark as its own toggle within Gemini — you can’t program the iPhone’s Activity Button to go directly to Spark, which is separate from Gemini’s chatbot interface. How great it would be if everything Gemini does was in a single destination! Ugh!) [...] And while Spark will later be able to do more with MCP integrations, not being able to set it to perform certain tasks, like booking your favorite date night restaurant regularly through Resy or looking for flight deals on a preferred booking engine, for instance, makes Spark feel somewhat lacking for the time being, given that not everything you do online takes place in Google’s universe of services.

(Also, I’d really like to text Spark. I wish that were an option, too.)
